preview_text:
The model rates Senegal as the clear favorite with a 60% win probability, leaving Iraq at 13% to emerge victorious. The gap of roughly 47 percentage points reflects a substantial quality separation in this fixture on a neutral ground.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 27%, suggesting the match is unlikely to be entirely one-sided. While Senegal holds a commanding edge, the relatively high draw probability points to a competitive contest where Iraq could secure a result short of victory. The 60–27–13 split indicates a favorable scenario for the favorite but with enough uncertainty to prevent dismissal of either an impasse or an upset.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both position Senegal notably higher than the model does, at 67–68% compared to 60%. The market diverges by roughly 7 percentage points in Senegal's favor, while treating Iraq nearly identically and shading the draw slightly lower. The dashboard does not assess which source is correct; bettors may interpret the gap as the model seeing modest edge value on Iraq or the books pricing in factors beyond the statistical forecast.
